title: citrus trifoliata
intro: Citrus trifoliata, also known as the bitter orange or trifoliate orange, is a small to medium-sized evergreen tree or shrub that belongs to the Rutaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat digestive issues due to its high content of flavonoids, limonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Citrus trifoliata include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which may help to prevent certain diseases such as cardiovascular disease and cancer. Herbal preparations of Citrus trifoliata can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat a range of health conditions, including colds, flu, and gastrointestinal issues.
